title: [pycde] ESI error
body:
When I use pycde.esi.DeclareRandomAccessMemory to generate multi-port ram, it produces the following error:

```
Assertion failed: toServerReq && toClientReq, file C:\__w\1\s\CIRCT\circt\lib\Dialect\ESI\ESIServices.cpp, line 171
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace.

```

OS: Window 10
pycde version: 0.2.1.dev823
The python code is:

```python
from pycde import (Clock, Input, Module, System, generator, types)
from pycde.constructs import Wire
from pycde import esi

RamI32x32 = esi.DeclareRandomAccessMemory(types.i32, 32, "RamI32x32")
WriteType = RamI32x32.write.to_server_type

@esi.ServiceDecl
class MP_RAM_io:
    write = esi.FromServer(WriteType)
    read = esi.ToFromServer(to_server_type=types.i32, to_client_type=types.i5)
    read1 = esi.ToFromServer(to_server_type=types.i32, to_client_type=types.i5)

class MP_RAM(Module):
    clk = Clock()
    rst = Input(types.i1)

    @generator
    def construct(io):
        RamI32x32.write(MP_RAM_io.write("write"))
        read_address = Wire(RamI32x32.read.to_server_type)
        read_data = RamI32x32.read(read_address)
        read_address.assign(MP_RAM_io.read(read_data, "read"))

        read1_address = Wire(RamI32x32.read.to_server_type)
        read1_data = RamI32x32.read(read1_address)
        read1_address.assign(MP_RAM_io.read1(read1_data, "read1"))

        RamI32x32.instantiate_builtin("sv_mem", [], inputs=[io.clk, io.rst])

mod = System([MP_RAM],name="test_mp_ram")
mod.compile()

```
